Enroll — Individual Enroll — Family Patient Portal Login

Wayne, PA · Philadelphia Main Line

What Is Direct Primary Care?

Primary care freed from insurance billing — and built around you. At Forward Family Medicine, DPC isn't a budget alternative to concierge medicine. It's the financial structure that makes concierge-quality care available to everyone.

≤250 patients per physician — capped
1–2 hr typical visit length
$200/mo individual membership
$0 copays, ever
Membership Benefits

What DPC Covers at Forward Family Medicine

✓ Included in Membership

  • Unlimited office visits (in-person, phone, video)
  • Same-day or next-day scheduling
  • Direct physician access — call, text, message
  • Annual physicals and preventive screenings
  • Acute illness care (infections, injuries, etc.)
  • Chronic disease management
  • Prescription refills and medication management
  • Lifestyle medicine and health coaching
  • Minor in-office procedures

+ At-Cost Add-Ons

  • Lab work at wholesale/near-cost pricing
  • Medications dispensed at cost — no pharmacy markup
  • Immunizations at cost

✕ Not Covered by DPC

  • Specialist visits (billed through your insurance)
  • Hospital care and emergency services
  • Imaging — we help coordinate and advocate for you

See the full services list →

Transparent Pricing

Wholesale Lab Pricing — What You Actually Pay

These are the prices you pay. Not an insurance "negotiated" rate. Not a facility fee. Just the lab cost — passed directly to you.

$2 CBC Complete Blood Count
$2 CMP Comprehensive Metabolic Panel
$3 Lipid Panel Total cholesterol, LDL, HDL, triglycerides
$7 ApoB Advanced cardiovascular risk marker
$7 Thyroid Panel TSH + additional thyroid markers
+ more Full menu available at your visit

Medications are also dispensed at cost through direct supplier relationships. No pharmacy markup.

The Comparison

How DPC Is Different from Traditional Primary Care

Feature Traditional Primary Care Forward Family Medicine (DPC)
Payment Insurance + copays + deductibles Flat monthly membership fee
Visit length 10–15 minutes (typical) 1–2 hours
Doctor access Via scheduling + nurse line Direct — call, text, message
Wait for appointment Days to weeks Same or next day
Patient panel size 1,500–2,500 patients ~250 patients per physician
After-hours access Answering service / urgent care Your own doctor
Preventive focus Often squeezed out Central to every visit
Real Numbers

What Does DPC Actually Cost You?

Most DPC members pair their membership with a high-deductible health plan (HDHP) for specialist and hospital coverage. The combination typically costs less than a traditional PPO — and you get far more.

Traditional PPO Only

$400–600/mo

  • 7–10 minute visits
  • Wait days to weeks
  • Still paying copays on top
  • 2,000+ patient panel
Better Value

DPC Membership + HDHP

$150–250/mo combined

  • 1–2 hour visits
  • Same-day access
  • No copays on primary care
  • ≤250 patient panel

Estimates vary by age, employer, and plan. Consult a benefits advisor for your situation.

Want to See If DPC Is Right for You?

Start with a free, no-obligation meet and greet. Meet the physicians, ask every question, and decide with zero pressure.

Schedule a Free Meet & Greet See Membership Plans →
Who It's For

Is DPC Right for You?

👨‍👩‍👧‍👦

Families & Individuals

Without employer-sponsored insurance who want excellent primary care at a predictable monthly cost.

🩺

Chronic Condition Management

Diabetes, hypertension, thyroid disorders — conditions that need ongoing, attentive management — not a 10-minute slot every 3 months.

🥗

Prevention-Focused Patients

Who want real time with their doctor to work on root causes, lifestyle changes, and long-term health goals.

💼

Self-Employed & Small Business

Looking for a cost-effective health benefit that delivers concierge-quality care without the concierge price tag.

Common Questions

Frequently Asked Questions

"Direct" refers to the direct financial relationship between patient and physician — you pay your doctor directly, without an insurance company in the middle. This removes administrative overhead and aligns incentives around your health, not billing volume.

DPC membership fees are not covered by Medicare or Medicaid. However, Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries can join a DPC practice as cash-paying members. Specialty and hospital services would still be billed through Medicare or Medicaid as applicable.

The distinction most practices draw between DPC and concierge medicine doesn't apply at Forward Family Medicine. Every member receives what the industry calls a "concierge experience" — delivered through the DPC financial model: no insurance billing, predictable monthly pricing. Learn more about our concierge features →

It covers the vast majority of what most people need from a primary care physician. You only pay extra for labs, medications, and immunizations — and even then, at cost. No per-visit fees, ever.

That's exactly what our free Meet & Greet is for. Schedule one here → It's a 20-minute conversation — in person or by video — with no pressure and no commitment.

More questions? See our full FAQ →

Take the Next Step

See the practice, meet Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman, and ask every DPC question you have — no obligation, no pressure.

Forward Family Medicine is a Direct Primary Care practice. Membership is not insurance. We recommend pairing DPC with a high-deductible or health-sharing plan.
